code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test07-JUL-2017Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, mild hyperchloremia, and significant proteinuria and glucosuria with low urine specific gravity. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but negative for nitrites, leukocytes, and blood. These findings suggest impaired renal function, possibly chronic kidney disease with tubular dysfunction (e.g., Fanconi syndrome), but no clear evidence of urinary tract infection. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
